title: Hook I/O protocol types
purpose: Shell command hooks need a standardized JSON protocol for communicating with external processes. This brief defines the HookInput struct (serialized to stdin) with common and per-event-type fields, the HookOutput struct (deserialized from stdout) with decision mapping, and the environment variable constants. NH-005 (ShellCommandHook) uses these types to serialize context and parse decisions.
task: Create crates/norn/src/integration/hooks/input.rs with HookInput and per-event-type field structs, all Serialize. Create crates/norn/src/integration/hooks/output.rs with HookOutput, decision parsing, and HookOutcome mapping. Define environment variable name constants. Update hooks/mod.rs with pub mod declarations and re-exports.

requirements.title: HookInput common fields
requirements.spec: HookInput SHALL contain common fields present for all event types: session_id, cwd, hook_event_name, agent_id, profile_name. All fields SHALL serialize to snake_case JSON keys.
acceptance: HookInput struct with fields: session_id (String), cwd (String), hook_event_name (String), agent_id (String), profile_name (String)
Derives Serialize, Debug, Clone
Serializes to flat JSON object with snake_case keys
Test: serialize HookInput, verify all common fields present in JSON output

requirements.title: Tool-event HookInput fields
requirements.spec: WHEN the hook event is a tool event (pre_tool, post_tool, post_tool_failure), HookInput SHALL include tool_name, tool_input, and tool_call_id fields. These SHALL be flattened into the same JSON object as the common fields.
acceptance: tool_name: Option<String> — present for tool events, None otherwise
tool_input: Option<serde_json::Value> — the tool's arguments
tool_call_id: Option<String> — provider-assigned tool call ID
Fields serialize as snake_case alongside common fields (not nested)
Option fields absent from JSON when None (serde skip_serializing_if)
Test: tool-event HookInput includes tool_name, tool_input, tool_call_id

requirements.title: Post-tool HookInput fields
requirements.spec: WHEN the hook event is post_tool or post_tool_failure, HookInput SHALL additionally include tool_output, tool_duration_ms, and tool_is_error fields.
acceptance: tool_output: Option<serde_json::Value> — the tool's output value
tool_duration_ms: Option<u64> — execution duration in milliseconds
tool_is_error: Option<bool> — whether the output represents an error
Test: post-tool HookInput includes all tool fields plus output fields

requirements.title: LLM, stop, and subagent HookInput fields
requirements.spec: HookInput SHALL include per-event-type fields for LLM events (model, message_count), stop events (final_text), and subagent events (subagent_id, subagent_type). All are Optional and absent from JSON when None.
acceptance: model: Option<String> — present for pre_llm, post_llm
message_count: Option<usize> — present for pre_llm, post_llm
final_text: Option<String> — present for stop events
subagent_id: Option<String> — present for subagent events
subagent_type: Option<String> — present for subagent events
Test: LLM-event HookInput includes model and message_count
Test: stop-event HookInput includes final_text
Test: subagent-event HookInput includes subagent_id and subagent_type

requirements.title: HookOutput deserialization and decision mapping
requirements.spec: HookOutput SHALL deserialize from JSON stdout of a shell hook. WHEN the decision field is "proceed" or absent, map to HookOutcome::Proceed. WHEN "block", map to HookOutcome::Block with reason. WHEN "modify" (pre_tool only), map to HookOutcome::Modify with updated_input. IF decision is "modify" and updated_input is missing, THEN treat as a config error logged at warn level and map to Proceed.
acceptance: HookOutput struct with fields: decision (Option<String>), reason (Option<String>), updated_input (Option<serde_json::Value>)
Derives Deserialize, Debug, Clone
to_hook_outcome(&self, event_type: HookEventType) -> HookOutcome method
decision=None or "proceed" → Proceed
decision="block" → Block { reason: self.reason.unwrap_or_default() }
decision="modify" with updated_input present → Modify { updated_input }
decision="modify" without updated_input → Proceed (logged at warn)
decision="modify" for non-pre_tool events → Proceed (logged at warn)
Test: all decision paths produce correct HookOutcome

requirements.title: Environment variable constants
requirements.spec: The hooks module SHALL define string constants for the 5 environment variables set on shell hook child processes: NORN_PROJECT_DIR, NORN_SESSION_ID, NORN_AGENT_ID, NORN_PROFILE, NORN_HOOK_EVENT.
acceptance: 5 pub const &str constants defined in a visible location (input.rs or a dedicated constants section)
NORN_PROJECT_DIR, NORN_SESSION_ID, NORN_AGENT_ID, NORN_PROFILE, NORN_HOOK_EVENT
Re-exported from hooks/mod.rs
Test: verify constants are non-empty and have expected values

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement shell command execution — that is NH-005
SHALL NOT implement settings file loading — that is NH-003
SHALL NOT wire these types into the agent loop — that is NH-005 and NH-007
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: HookInput serializes to JSON with all common fields present — verified by test
HookOutput decision mapping covers all paths — verified by test
Environment variable constants are re-exported from hooks module
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
